generated_oml:
instance MqttRobotTelemetryStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Automated one-way data flow from physical serial robot (MABI MAX 100) to digital representation using MQTT protocol for IoT and real-time data transmission. Collects contextual data traces including sensor readings and operational logs without explicit data type specification."
]

instance RobotJointAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor component capturing joint angle measurements for the serial robot in manufacturing automation context."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MqttRobotTelemetryStream
]

instance RobotJointTorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor component capturing joint torque measurements for the serial robot in manufacturing automation context."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MqttRobotTelemetryStream
]

instance RobotEndEffectorPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor component capturing end effector position measurements for the serial robot in manufacturing automation context."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MqttRobotTelemetryStream
]

instance RobotOperationalLogCollector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Component collecting operational logs and system state data from the serial robot's control system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MqttRobotTelemetryStream
]

instance SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based functional architecture model representing requirements, functions, and logical decomposition of the serial robot system using RFLP methodology."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLIntegrationEnabler, MontiArcIntegrationEnabler
]

instance SysMLLogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based logical architecture model defining component interfaces, interconnections, and composition for the serial robot system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLIntegrationEnabler, MontiArcIntegrationEnabler
]

instance MontiArcComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc architecture model defining component-based system decomposition and interface specifications for the serial robot."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcIntegrationEnabler
]

instance MontiArcBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton model for behavioral description of the serial robot's dynamic behaviors and state transitions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcIntegrationEnabler
]

instance SimulinkDynamicBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for detailed behavioral modeling and dynamic system simulation of robot control logic and physics-based behaviors."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkIntegrationEnabler
]

instance RobotContextualDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces from MQTT telemetry stream including sensor readings, operational logs, and system state data for the serial robot."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MqttRobotTelemetryStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance RobotSimulationInputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed data derived from contextual traces used as input for simulation and predictive analytics of the serial robot."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MqttRobotTelemetryStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkIntegrationEnabler
]

instance RobotHistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical system state and performance data collected over time for trend analysis and predictive maintenance of the serial robot."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MqttRobotTelemetryStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance SysMLIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for integrating SysML models (functional and logical architectures) with other modeling tools and simulation environments."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance MontiArcIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for integrating MontiArc component and behavioral models with simulation tools and execution environments."

]

instance SimulinkIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for integrating Simulink dynamic behavior models with data processing pipelines and control logic implementation."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for processing contextual data traces including filtering, normalization, and feature extraction for analytics."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for creating stakeholder-specific visualizations using SysML IBDs and MontiArc component diagrams."

]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time state tracking and contextual data aggregation from the physical serial robot to its digital representation."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobotStateInsight, OperationalTraceInsight

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in this context is orchestrated through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies**, which integrate multiple modeling notations and tools to describe the system’s structure and behavior. The architecture follows **component & connector-based patterns** (e.g., ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML IBDs), where components represent physical or logical entities (e.g., sensors, actuators, or functional modules), and connectors define their **interfaces, interconnections, and composition**. Coordination mechanisms are implicit in these architectures, relying on **formal descriptions of interfaces** and **behavioral models** (e.g., state machines in SysML v1.7 or MontiArc Automaton) to ensure system-wide consistency. Resource management is not explicitly detailed, but it may involve **tool-specific mechanisms** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink for simulation or general-purpose languages like C++/Java for execution). The constellation may also incorporate **heterogeneous tools and stakeholders**, requiring integration frameworks to handle disparate modeling notations and perspectives (e.g., requirements, functional, logical, and physical layers)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle stages, as inferred from the provided document, align with **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies and involve **multi-level abstraction phases** to manage complexity. These stages include:
  1. **Requirements Phase**: Defines functional and non-functional requirements for the Digital Twin, serving as the foundational input for subsequent stages.
  2. **Functional Architecture Phase**: Utilizes **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, or MontiArc** to model components, interfaces, and interconnections, abstracting the system’s logical behavior.
  3. **Logical Architecture Phase**: Refines the functional architecture into a **component & connector-based** structure, often employing **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** or **MontiArc Automaton** to define state machines and dynamic interactions.
  4. **Product/Technical Layer Phase**: Implements the Digital Twin using **simulation tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**) or **general-purpose programming languages** (e.g., **C++, Java**) to operationalize the model, ensuring alignment with physical system behavior.
  Transition management between phases is facilitated by **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **tool interoperability**, as MBSE requires synthesis of models across different notations (e.g., SysML for architecture, Simulink for behavior). Lifecycle governance is implicitly tied to **methodologies like RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics), OOSEM, SPES, or SMArDT**, which provide structured frameworks for evolution and validation. However, explicit governance approaches (e.g., compliance frameworks, version control protocols) are not detailed in the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **fidelity and validity considerations** through structured **validation and verification (V&V) methods** tied to MBSE practices. Key aspects include:
  - **Model fidelity requirements** are addressed via **multi-layered abstraction** (e.g., functional, logical, physical) and **tool-specific validation** (e.g., SysML state machines, Simulink simulations).
  - **Uncertainty sources** are implicitly managed through **data aggregation and abstraction** of contextual traces, though explicit quantification methods (e.g., probabilistic models or error propagation analysis) are not detailed in the provided context.
  - **Error detection and correction mechanisms** rely on **simulation-driven feedback loops** (e.g., comparing simulated behavior with real-world data) and **tool-chain consistency checks** across heterogeneous modeling environments (e.g., SysML ↔ Simulink).
  - **Quality assurance processes** are inferred from the integration of **stakeholder perspectives** and **heterogeneous data sources**, ensuring traceability from requirements to implementation. However, specific metrics (e.g., accuracy thresholds, precision tolerances) or formal verification protocols (e.g., model checking) are not explicitly described."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The document explicitly references **MQTT** as the communication protocol for data transmission between the physical and digital layers in the Digital Shadow context. However, no further details are provided regarding **network topology/architecture**, **bandwidth/latency requirements**, **reliability/redundancy mechanisms**, or **security protocols** (e.g., encryption, authentication, or access control). The mention of **heterogeneous tools and stakeholders** in MBSE (e.g., SysML, Simulink, C++/Java) does not imply specific network infrastructure requirements for the Digital Twin system itself. Thus, the **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Twin is **not described** beyond the use of MQTT for data streaming."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document specifies integration with **external modeling tools and simulation environments**, such as MathWorks Simulink, other simulation tools, and general-purpose programming languages (e.g., C++, Java). Data exchange is implied through **MQTT** (a lightweight publish-subscribe protocol) for streaming time-series data, as referenced in the Digital Shadow context. While **interoperability standards** like **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** are not explicitly detailed for Digital Twins, the system relies on **formal modeling languages (SysML, ADLs)** and **tool integration** to ensure compatibility across heterogeneous components. The document does not explicitly mention **API-based communication** or interactions with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems, but it emphasizes **modeling tool interoperability** as a key aspect."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **standardized modeling languages and frameworks**, including **SysML** (for systems engineering), **ACME**, **AADL**, and **MontiArc** (for architecture description), as well as **UML** (for class diagrams, e.g., Digital Shadow Reference Model). Interoperability is supported through **Asset Administration Shells** (AAS), though specific **ISO standards (e.g., ISO/IEC 19505 for SysML, ISO/IEC 42010 for architecture descriptions)** or **XMI (XML Metadata Interchange)** are not explicitly named in the provided documents. Compliance frameworks and certification requirements are not detailed, nor are **interoperability protocols** beyond the use of standardized languages and tools. The focus remains on **methodological consistency** (e.g., MBSE, ADLs) rather than formal certification or cross-domain standardization."
]
